description_long: This is a cylindrical flute with a segmented, striped pattern featuring alternating bands of green, red, white, and brown. The flute appears to be crafted from wood, with visible grain and a matte finish, and is decorated with dark circular holes along one side, likely serving as finger holes. The coloration is uneven, suggesting hand-painted or dyed sections, giving it a rustic, artisanal appearance. The ends of the flute are rounded, and the instrument is relatively slender and straight. There are no visible metallic parts or key mechanisms, indicating a simple, traditional design. The surface shows some wear, adding to its authentic, handmade character.
description: A slender, hand-painted wooden flute with alternating green, red, white, and brown bands and visible finger holes. The design is rustic and artisanal, with a matte finish and no visible keys.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the flute with its alternating colored bands and no visible holes.
description_view_1: End-on view showing the circular cross-section and the alignment of the colored bands.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the finger holes along the length and the segmented color pattern.
description_view_3: End-on view from the opposite side, highlighting the rounded end and colored segments.
